14.reason: [The condition uses '<' instead of '<=' for upper bounds (e.g., s[i+1]<'z' should be s[i+1]<='z'), and 's[i+1]<'1'' is incorrect鈥攊t should be 's[i+1]<='9''. Also, accessing s[i-1] when i=0 leads to out-of-bounds read.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Boundary conditions are incorrect: should use <= 'z', <= 'Z', <= '9'; also '1' is wrong, should be '9']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Boundary conditions are incorrect: should use <= 'z', <= 'Z', and <= '9'; also '1' is wrong, should be '9']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition uses '<' instead of '<=' for upper bounds (e.g., s[i+1]<'z' should be s[i+1]<='z'), and 's[i+1]<'1'' is incorrect鈥攊t should be 's[i+1]<='9''. Also, accessing s[i-1] when i=0 leads to out-of-bounds read.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  // The condition uses '<' instead of '<=' for upper bounds (e.g., 'z', 'Z', '9'), so valid ranges like 'a-z' or '0-9' are incorrectly excluded. Also, 's[i+1]<'1'' is wrong鈥攊t should be '<='9'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition uses '<' instead of '<=' for upper bounds (e.g., s[i+1]<'z'), which excludes valid end characters like 'z', 'Z', and '9'. Also, the digit range check uses '1' instead of '9'.]

18.reason: [Loop condition is off-by-one; it should be c[j] < s[i+1] to include the correct range. Also, if s[i+1] is only one greater than s[i-1], this loop won't run but should output nothing between, yet logic may still misbehave.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop condition is off by one; should be c[j] < s[i+1]]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop condition is off by one; should be c[j] < s[i+1] to include the correct range]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop condition is off-by-one; it should be c[j] < s[i+1] to include the correct range. Also, if s[i+1] is only one greater than s[i-1], this loop won't run but should output nothing between, yet logic may still misbehave.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  // This loop condition causes under-expansion. For example, expanding 'a-d' would stop at 'c' instead of 'd'. It should be `while(c[j] < s[i+1])`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop condition should be c[j] < s[i+1], not c[j] < s[i+1]-1, otherwise it stops one character too early.]
